request: Design the VIP Marina 'Weekdays on the Water' landing page from a brand-new brief.

inputs.ref: client brief call + existing brand deck

brief.deliverable: Single-file HTML/CSS landing page, desktop + mobile

hardNos.why: brand voice: never generic link text

options.summary: Trust-first: local fleet, plain pricing, calm structure.
Loss-aversion hero: 'Summer is 12 weekends' with the boat picker promoted to the hero.
Boat-picker-first: pricing and picker above the fold, trust signals below.

optionFeedback.note: keep the loss-aversion line, drop the picker-as-hero framing
picker-first reads as a rental marketplace, not VIP

evidence.statement: Read the client brief call notes and the current marina.com/rentals page this session before drafting.
Ran scripts/validate-output.py against outputs/vip-marina-landing.html and examples/lock-fixture.json this session.
Output audit: 4 colors found, all in lock; no Hard-NO hits.
Spacing/grid and logo-usage lock fields are not machine-checked in v1.0; verified by eye only.

scorecard.evidence: Both breakpoints present, screenshotted this session
Output audit: all colors in lock
File path shared, opens in any browser

verdict.reason: Brief was complete before generation, three distinct lock-compliant concepts were shown, concept B (revised per keep/kill/change feedback) was built, and the output audit passed clean.
verdict.requiredProof: Output audit pass + full design scorecard.
